zh.lifeSummary: Muriel de Chappell是Eudo de Chapel的女兒，於大約1040年出生在法國巴-諾門迪可騰。她和Lord Tickhill Roger de Busli至少有6個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於大約1123年在英國薩默塞特享年85歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chappell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived near a chapel, from Middle English chapel(l)e ‘chapel’, via Old French, from Late Latin capella, originally a diminutive of capa ‘hood, cloak’, but later transferred to the sense ‘chapel, sanctuary’, with reference to the shrine at Tours where the cloak of Saint Martin was preserved as a relic.

variants.name: Capell
meanings: English: variant of Chappell ‘chapel’, from a Norman form with hard c-, applied as a topographic or occupational name, or as a habitational name for someone from any of several minor places called with this word, such as Capel in Surrey, Capel le Ferne in Kent, or Capel Saint Andrew and Capel Saint Mary in Suffolk.

English: from Middle English capel, caple, capul(le) ‘stallion, gelding; warhorse, cart horse, riding horse’, denoting someone thought to resemble a horse in some way or possibly someone whose occupation involved horses.
Catalan: from capell ‘hat, hood’, as a nickname for someone who habitually wore a hat or hood, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who made hats or hoods.

variants.name: Chappelle
meanings: English and Scottish (Stirlingshire): variant of   Chappell   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
French: variant of Chapelle and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Chappelle is rare in France.

variants.name: Chapel
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Chappell  .

French: from a diminutive of Old French chape ‘hooded cloak, cape, hood’, or ‘hat’ (from Late Latin capa), hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of cloaks or hats, or a nickname for a habitual wearer of a distinctive cloak or hat.

nameMeanings.name: Muriel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin ( see Muireall ). Forms of the name are found in Breton as well as in Irish and Scottish Gaelic, and in the form Meriel it was in use in the heart of England in the Middle Ages. The surname Merrill is derived from it. As Muriel it was at first used mainly in Scotland, but became more widely popular during the first half of the 20th century. Since then it has fallen from favour again.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Muriel de Chappell was born about 1040, in Cotentin, Lower Normandy, France as the daughter of Eudo de Chapel. She had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with Lord Tickhill Roger de Busli. She died about 1123, in Somerset, England, at the age of 85, and was buried in Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Muriel de Chappellは約1040年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディコテンティンでEudo de Chapelの娘として生まれた。彼女はLord Tickhill Roger de Busliとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1123年にイギリスソメルセトで85歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Muriel de Chappell(은)는 대략 1040년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 컷틴에서 Eudo de Chapel의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Lord Tickhill Roger de Busli(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1123년 영국 서머세트에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Muriel de Chappell родилась около 1040 г. в местности котентинь, бассе-норманди, Франция. Её отца звали Eudo de Chapel. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь от Lord Tickhill Roger de Busli. Она умерла около 1123 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности суффолк, Англия.
